behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on real git rename detection and the CLI’s externally visible stdout contract (category assignment/counts). A unit-only parser test can miss failures from real repo state + git output shape + CLI wiring. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML model call is mentioned, and no multi-agent sequence is described.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit baseline files, perform a rename, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert stdout report shows the file only in `renamed` (not in `added` or `deleted`) and process exits successfully.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires real git + real CLI stdout verification, which is System-level.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly checks crash behavior and output when real binary diffs are present. Mocked diff text often hides real subprocess/encoding edge cases. The behavior is an external CLI happy-path/error-avoidance contract (no crash, correct count). Rejected higher levels: behavior text has no AI/ML dependency and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add/modify a binary file, run the CLI subprocess, assert exit code is success, no crash output, and binary file is included in counts/category in stdout report.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test validates real subprocess behavior and stdout contract; lower levels can miss binary handling failures.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: No-commit repos trigger real git edge behavior (e.g., unborn HEAD semantics) that stubs frequently simplify. The behavior asserts an external CLI contract: empty report instead of failure. Rejected higher levels: no model output quality or agentic sequencing is part of the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with zero commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert successful exit and stdout equals the empty-report structure (and not an error).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real git repository state and end-to-end CLI observation.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is a round-trip reality check against actual repository history, not just internal counting logic. Unit tests can validate algorithms but can miss divergence between real git history extraction and report output. Rejected higher levels: behavior text contains no AI/ML decision-making and no chained agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known added/deleted/renamed/modified events across commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert reported counts exactly match the known ground truth from that real history.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Uses real infrastructure and externally visible output to verify end-to-end correctness.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics depend on real git index/worktree behavior and command invocation details. Mocking can hide failures in how the CLI queries git state. The behavior validates an external output contract across real repository states. Rejected higher levels: no AI/ML output dependency and no multi-step agentic pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run CLI subprocess for each scenario, and assert stdout reports are identical.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Needs real git state transitions and CLI output comparison, which is System-level.
